reviewer.evidencePolicy: Do not satisfy broad adapter-gate operating rules with one eval. Split evaluator, review-variant, CLI-discovery, and agent-surface readiness into concrete proof obligations before planning fixtures.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim is a repo operating rule spanning evaluator proof, review-variant proof, CLI-discovery proof, and agent-surface proof. Treating it as one dev/repo eval target would overclaim because each requested gate has a different readiness command and failure mode.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Current doctor, agent-status, review-variant, and evaluator commands can prove individual readiness surfaces, but this broad rule does not name a single selected gate or adapter capability. It should drive decomposition, not a single eval fixture.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Split this operating rule into concrete checkable claims: selected evaluator command readiness, review variants availability, CLI-discovery command/help availability, and agent-surface doctor/readiness. Attach evidence to those split claims when each selected gate is actually requested.
unresolvedQuestions: Which release or quality gate should own each split readiness claim?
Should the split claims live as maintainer-facing specdown docs, adapter contract docs, or repo-local quality adapter assertions?
